content.assessment: Partial phase-down of only the age-10 entry point requires legislative amendment to the statutory provisions in Act CXC governing 6-year gimnázium tracks specifically, while leaving 8-year and 4-year entry points and their governing law untouched. This narrows the legal-authority burden relative to full structural reform, but the same maintainer-autonomy constraint applies to any 6-year track operated by church or private maintainers, and the incremental nature invites the same 'precedent for further transformation' framing seen with narrower admissions proposals.
opportunities: Could serve as a legally lower-risk test case for maintainer negotiation processes needed for any future broader reform
strengths: Narrower amendment scope relative to full delay-to-14 reform, targeting only the 6-year entry provision within the existing statutory framework [F-live-legal_and_governance-01]
threats: Political framing risk observed in narrower admissions-review proposals suggests any partial structural change may be characterized as precedent for further unilateral action, straining maintainer trust regardless of the proposal's actual legal scope
weaknesses: Still requires negotiated agreement with church and private maintainers operating 6-year tracks, since they fall outside Klebelsberg Centre authority [F-live-legal_and_governance-03]
Evidence base for this proposal is characterized as weak, limiting confidence in governance design assumptions
